title: Palleus Chuff
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type= |image= |name=Palleus Chuff |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'' |pronouns=new |height=Less than 1 meter |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation= |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|It's Ventress! She's come here to kill you. She took me prisoner, thinking I was you, but somehow she found out you were going to be here, and she's come for the real thing. She made one miscalculation, though. Left me alone in the ship. Didn't think I could do her any harm, oh no! Not Chuff the puny actor. But I have programmed her horrible ship to self-destruct!|Palleus Chuff to Yoda|}} '''Palleus Chuff''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Actor/Legends|actor]] during the reign of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Since childhood, his passion was pretending; specifically, pretending to be a hero. His small height, which was below one meter, forced him to portray dubious characters in his early acting career. However, he eventually made his breakthrough with his portrayal of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] in his self-written play ''[[Jedi!]]''. Later, in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], Chuff impersonated Yoda at the Jedi Master's behest on a personal mission to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ithor/Legends|Ithor]] in the [[Ottega system/Legends|Ottega]] [[Star system/Legends|system]]. The goal of this mission was to keep Yoda's [[Mission to Vjun (Clone Wars)|mission]] to [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]] secret. Arriving in the Ottega system, Chuff was attacked by the [[Last Call (Ginivex-class)|''Last Call'']], the personal [[Starship/Legends|starship]] of the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]], who erroneously believed him to be Yoda. After a short time, Ventress discovered that Yoda had not flown the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], and she changed her plan to confuse her enemies by faking Yoda's death and holding Chuff captive so that he could not tell anyone the truth. A few days later, after a business transaction with the [[FIII Footman droid|FIII Footman]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[Solis]], Ventress found out that the real Yoda was going to the [[Phindar/Legends|Phindar]] [[Phindar Spaceport|Spaceport]]. Considering the actor harmless, she left him in the ship when she [[Battle of the Phindar Spaceport|tried]] to kill the Jedi Master. However, Chuff engaged her ship's [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] for a random [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] flight. Outside the ship, the actor was confronted with the real Yoda. As the ship was anchored to the deck with five [[High-pull magnet|high-pull]] [[Magnet/Legends|magnets]], Yoda knew that the firing of the engines would tear the whole [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] apart. As a Jedi, Yoda's main goal was to destroy the magnets holding the ship in place to prevent the spaceport from this catastrophe. As he neared completion, [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] tried to attack Yoda, forcing him to trust Chuff with the mission while he engaged the droids. At that point, they split up, and Chuff succeeded in destroying the last magnet by himself, saving the spaceport. ==Biography== ===Early career=== [[File:Yoda1280.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Jedi Master Yoda]] Palleus Chuff spent most of his childhood taking part in role-playing games. In those games he often played [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]], or adventurers. From childhood on, he was afraid of enclosed spaces and of flying. Because he loved role-playing so much, he decided to become an [[Actor/Legends|actor]]. As he was very short—less than one meter—the producers would not risk casting him as a hero. Due to this, he usually found himself playing villainous, scheming dwarfs. Disappointed by this fact, he came up with a play in which he portrayed the diminutive [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]. Chuff studied Yoda's mannerisms for several months to be able to portray him realistically in the play ''[[Jedi!]]'' After this, he was capable of using Yoda's emotions according to the particular situation. The effort paid off, with the play getting rave reviews from the media and earning him much respect with his fellow actors. ===Impersonating Yoda=== {{Quote|Make a feint. Tell everyone you are leaving. Make a show of it, Master. Show pictures of you getting into a Jedi starfighter."
"—But the pictures are a deception. While the world watches you go on a very public mission, in reality you will slip onto a different ship with us. A clever idea, boy.|Whie Malreaux and Jai Maruk on how to smuggle Yoda out of Coruscant|}} In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Jedi Master Yoda was on a [[Mission to Vjun (Clone Wars)|mission]] with Masters [[Maks Leem]] and [[Jai Maruk]], and their respective [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] [[Whie Malreaux/Legends|Whie Malreaux]] and [[Tallisibeth Enwandung-Esterhazy]], to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]]. The group was meeting [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], the leader of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], for a peace talk. At the same time, Chuff was employed to masquerade as Yoda on a mission to the planet [[Ithor/Legends|Ithor]] in the [[Ottega system/Legends|Ottega]] [[Star system/Legends|system]] at the request of the government of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. The actor was to travel to Ithor within a [[Seltaya-class fast courier|''Seltaya''-class fast courier]], a type of starfighter manufactured by [[Hoersch-Kessel Drive, Inc./Legends|Hoersch-Kessel Drive]]. Four [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] escorted him within [[Tavya-class armored picket|''Tavya''-class armored pickets]] to protect him in [[Starfighter combat/Legends|starfighter combat]]. The goal of this mission was to fool [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] into believing that the real Yoda was on the [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. Despite his fear of flying, Chuff agreed, because he found it disrespectful to deny a request from the government. However, he wanted a [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] to navigate his ''Seltaya''-class fast courier so that he would not have to worry about piloting the starfighter. Furthermore, there was an [[emergency override]] that would allow the [[Control tower/Legends|control tower]] to fly the starfighter. Shortly before the departure, he became nervous and imagined that his R2 droid had been manipulated by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], a member of the Confederacy. In the [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] upon his departure, Chuff did not have the heart to answer the questions posed by the local [[Journalist/Legends|journalists]], instead smiling and waving his walking stick at the reporters. [[File:Asajjventressheadshot-obsession.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Asajj Ventress, who kidnapped Chuff over Ithor]] The [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] decided to ambush Chuff and his fellow pilots, because she knew that a [[Death/Legends|dead]] Yoda—who was a beacon of hope for many citizens in the galaxy—would be a catastrophe for the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. Before Chuff arrived in the system, Ventress had already killed three of the four pilots. However, the actor did not care and commanded his astromech droid to fire upon Ventress' ship, the [[Last Call (Ginivex-class)|''Last Call'']]. Ventress sensed through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] that it was not Yoda flying the starfighter, and formulated a new plan to confuse her enemies by capturing Chuff and leaving the wreckage of his craft at the battle site. The Ithorians identified the debris as Yoda's and broadcasted the message of his death throughout the galaxy. As a captive in Ventress' ship, Chuff was gagged and tied to a seat. At one point, the [[FIII Footman droid]] [[Solis]] contacted Ventress. The droid had spied on one of Yoda's companions and meanwhile found out the Jedi's next stopover, which he offered to tell her for the price of 734,995 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Ventress was very interested in doing business with the droid, since the death of Yoda was still one of her prime concerns. ===Meeting Yoda=== {{Quote|By the stars. It's me! I mean, you!|Chuff meeting Yoda for the first time|}} When they arrived at Yoda's location, the [[Phindar Spaceport|spaceport]] on [[Phindar/Legends|Phindar]], Ventress perceived Chuff as harmless and left him in the ship. Seeking revenge, Chuff set a ten-minute countdown to engage the ship's [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] for a random [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] flight. When he got out of the ship, he was confronted by the real Yoda. Worried about the Jedi Master's safety, Chuff immediately informed him of the Dark Jedi's plans. He also mentioned the hyperdrive that he had manipulated shortly before. After hearing this, Yoda was slightly displeased with Chuff. As the ship was anchored to the deck with five [[High-pull magnet|high-pull]] [[Magnet/Legends|magnets]] on the bottom of the [[support leg]]s, an ignition of the hyperdrive engine could tear the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] apart. To avoid a devastating blast, Yoda attempted to chop down the supports legs one by one. Then, he intended to push the ship out of the docking bay so that the spaceport would be untroubled by the destruction. However, after Yoda had managed to destroy only four of the five legs, Chuff witnessed [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] trying to attack the Jedi Master. Yoda decided that it would make more sense if Chuff assumed the responsibility for the last support leg, while he went out to eliminate the attacking droids. Solely responsible for the future of the spaceport, Chuff's major goal was to not disappoint Yoda. After he successfully cleaved the last support leg—which was no serious challenge for him—the actor went directly to the [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolifts]] outside of the spaceport as Yoda had ordered him to. A few minutes later, Yoda came back to join Chuff, when suddenly suction from the ignition of the engines forced everything near the ship into space. Only Yoda and Chuff were able to keep their position, because Yoda used the Force to avoid such a disaster. ==Personality and traits== Palleus Chuff loved to play the role of the hero. Restricted by his height, he often had to portray scheming characters, which frustrated his ambitions. Therefore, he came up with making his breakthrough by writing his own play, entitled ''Jedi!''. To give an authentic portrayal of Jedi Master Yoda, he studied his mannerisms for several months. After this period of study, he had Yoda's emotions fairly mastered and could use them appropriately. The effort paid off, as the play's premiere proved to be a big success. In addition, his colleagues recognized his witty qualities. Since childhood, Chuff had a fear of flying as well as claustrophobia. Due to these fears, he prayed before the departure of his courier. However, in his mind, serving the government had a higher priority than avoiding his fears, so he accepted their mission. After the time he spent in Ventress' ship, the actor was annoyed with the Dark Jedi and decided to gain revenge by making her ship perform a random hyperspace jump. Chuff, who was convinced of his plan, saw—after Yoda's explanation—that his plan had some flaws that he had not considered. When Yoda and the actor had to cut the support legs, Chuff initially felt a shaky courage. However, he lost his gallantry just before the ignition of the engines, fearing his own death. Chuff eventually became more confident, when Yoda suddenly arrived to save him. ==Behind the scenes== Palleus Chuff's first and only appearance was in the [[2004]] novel ''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'', written by [[Sean Stewart]]. In ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', Chuff's first name is incorrectly spelled as ''Pelleus''.{{CSWECite|I|126|Chuff, Palleus}} ==Appearances== *''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]'' {{Ab|Yoda: Dark Rendezvous (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book and ''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous (abridged audiobook)|abridged audiobook]]''}} *[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous (abridged audiobook)|''Yoda: Dark Rendezvous'' abridged audiobook]] ==Sources== *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{ID|Pelleus}} *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |cs=Palleus Chuff |de=Palleus Chuff }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Chuff, Palleus}} [[Category:Actors]] [[Category:Impersonators]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified species]] [[Category:Males]]
